steps.expected: Explicit boundaries defining what UI.md is and is not
steps.observed: Table in 1.1 differentiates UI.md from DESIGN.md, PRD, ARCHITECTURE.md, API.md, Storybook
Section 1.3 explicitly lists UI.md covers (screens, navigation, states, etc.) and excludes (visual tokens, framework names, API paths)

evidence.quote:
UI.md **covers**: Screen inventory, Navigation graph, State model, Interaction patterns, Data contract, Machine-readable appendix
UI.md **excludes**: Visual design tokens, Framework-specific names, API endpoint paths, Code snippets, Marketing copy

steps.observed: Found 7 required sections: Screen Inventory (5), Navigation Model (6), Core User Flows (7), Interaction Patterns (8), State Model (9), Data Contract (10), Machine-Readable Appendix (11)

steps.expected: At least 5 required fields per screen
steps.observed: Schema defines 7 required fields: id, purpose, primaryActions, entryConditions, exitConditions, states, roleAccess
Section 5.4 provides complete example for SCREEN-board with all fields filled

evidence.exampleScreen: SCREEN-board with Purpose, Primary Actions, Entry Conditions, Exit Conditions, States

steps.observed: 6 state categories defined: loading, empty, error, success, offline, permission-denied

steps.expected: Behavioral descriptions for each state type
steps.observed: Section 9.4 provides detailed examples for STATE-loading, STATE-empty-board, STATE-error, STATE-offline, STATE-permission-denied, STATE-success

stateCategories: STATE-loading: Initial load, refresh, background sync
STATE-empty: No data, filtered empty, first-use
STATE-error: Network failure, validation error, permission denied, server error
STATE-offline: Disconnected, reconnecting, cached data stale
STATE-permission-denied: User lacks access to feature or data

steps.expected: Navigation spec with at least 3 example transitions
steps.observed: Section 6.2 provides 3+ example transitions in Markdown table and Mermaid diagram formats

steps.action: Verify entry points, terminal screens, conditional routing
steps.observed: Section 6.6 defines entry points, terminal screens, and conditional routing

exampleTransitions: SCREEN-login → SCREEN-board (Submit credentials, Auth success, No)
SCREEN-board → SCREEN-cardDetail (Click card, Card selected, Push)
evidence.mermaidExample: graph LR with A[SCREEN-login] --> B[SCREEN-board], B --> C[SCREEN-cardDetail], B --> D[SCREEN-filter]

steps.expected: At least 4 required attributes per data entity
steps.observed: Data entity schema has 5 required fields: entityId, name, fields, screenUsage, operations

steps.expected: Field-level schema with label, type, constraints
steps.observed: Field schema has 7 attributes: name, label, type, constraints, required, readOnly, format
Section 10.5 provides ENTITY-card and ENTITY-user examples

assertions.title: Machine-readable appendix specification

steps.expected: Appendix schema defined with validation rules, compatible with tooling/ui-md-linter/schema.json
steps.observed: SPEC.md defines comprehensive JSON Schema in section 11.3 with 7 validation rules in 11.4

steps.expected: Linter schema file exists and matches SPEC.md appendix schema
steps.observed: ISSUE: tooling/ui-md-linter/schema.json is empty ({}) - incompatible with the specification

evidence.specDefines: Full JSON Schema with version, screens, states, navigation, roles, dataContracts, and 7 validation rules
evidence.linterSchemaStatus: tooling/ui-md-linter/schema.json is empty ({}) - does not match the specification

assertions.issues: The SPEC.md defines a complete appendix schema, but the tooling/ui-md-linter/schema.json file is empty. This breaks compatibility between the specification and the linter tooling.

blockers.description: tooling/ui-md-linter/schema.json is empty - needs to be populated with the JSON Schema defined in SPEC.md section 11.3
blockers.quickFixAttempted: None - this requires code changes to the tooling, not just a documentation fix

summary: Tested 8 assertions (VAL-SPEC-001 through VAL-SPEC-008): 7 passed, 1 failed. VAL-SPEC-008 failed because tooling/ui-md-linter/schema.json is empty ({}) while SPEC.md section 11 defines a comprehensive machine-readable appendix schema. All other SPEC.md completeness and correctness assertions pass.
